meta.description: Does AI referencing past sessions improve the experience? Deep memory (specific grudges, running jokes) vs light memory (generic continuity) vs no memory (control). Tests whether multi-session context creates stickier engagement.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, did anyone actually see Pat on their phone? Because I didn't catch anything during setup.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's claiming without solid proof—this is the kind of weak play that ruins the integrity of the game, and I'm not voting for bullshit just because we're five sessions in.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat got caught! Okay okay, I'm voting yes because honestly who isn't checking their phone at game night—but also Pat you gotta commit to the bit more, we all saw you!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the energy I came for—the night is STARTING and people are already being dumb and getting caught, I love this group so much.

pat.dialogue: Whoa whoa, hold up — I literally just pulled my phone out to check the group chat about dessert timing. That's not... okay, that's actually pretty good, but come on, we're three minutes in.
pat.internal_thought: This is awkward because it's technically true but it feels cheap to claim on myself right at the start when no one's even settled in yet — and now I have to decide if I want to validate this or shut it down as host.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that tracks. Pat's been glued to that thing.
river.internal_thought: Pat definitely checked their phone during setup — I caught it in my peripheral. This is an easy one to vote on, but also... kind of a weak first play.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat, when exactly? I was watching the board the whole time during setup and didn't see anything. This feels like a stretch.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's hunting for easy points before the actual game even starts, and frankly this alibi system is going to waste five minutes on something I didn't even witness.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — you got me fair and square. Phone was out for like thirty seconds, but the alibi vote better be good because I'm about to use my host brain to make sure this doesn't become 'let's catch Pat on everything' night.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually smart gameplay — they're testing if I'll be defensive as the host, and honestly it's funny, but I need to set the tone that I'm judging this fairly or people will think I'm rigging things later.

river.dialogue: Pat's already claiming stuff? We're six minutes in.
river.internal_thought: Pat's being aggressive early, probably nervous energy—but The Alibi mechanic actually might make this interesting depending on who the witness is.

timeline.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
timeline.description: Who's most likely to forget this night?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who's most likely to forget this night?

marcus.dialogue: Who's most likely to forget? I don't know, ask me in two hours when we're actually three drinks deep. Right now we're literally six minutes in.
marcus.internal_thought: This poll is pointless noise during setup when I'm trying to focus on the actual game strategy—and it's asking people to vote on a social prediction that won't matter until the night's over.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, okay, this is PERFECT—I'm voting Pat because he's genuinely gonna black out first and wake up tomorrow like 'did we even play?' But also—wait, no, Marcus! Marcus is definitely gonna be like 'what game night?' in two weeks, I'm changing my vote—actually no, Pat, final answer Pat!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here, these people are hilarious and I get to mess with them for points, let's GOOOO.

tyler.dialogue: Oh damn, that's actually—wait, am I the answer? *laughs* That's fair.
tyler.internal_thought: This is kinda funny because they're definitely talking about me right now, and I'm literally the one who'll forget because I wasn't paying attention, so the joke's on me I guess.

pat.dialogue: Oh that's good timing — honestly? Probably Marcus, dude forgets his own birthday half the time. But I'm voting for River because they always say they remember everything and then text me three weeks later like 'wait what happened at game night?'
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect energy for minute 6 — everyone's still fresh, the vibe is light, and a poll about forgetting stuff is harmless enough that I can lean into the group dynamic without worrying about tension.

river.dialogue: Hmm. Probably me, honestly. I tune out half the games anyway.
river.internal_thought: Poll is lazy and kind of boring—asking who'll forget when we're literally six minutes in? And yeah, I'd pick myself because I genuinely won't remember half these rules by next week.

alex.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — okay, Marcus is definitely forgetting this by next week, he always does. I'm voting Marcus because that's the chaos play and also factually true.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of soft social read I can weaponize — everyone knows Marcus has a memory like a sieve, but voting for myself keeps me in the conversation and signals I'm in on the joke.

timeline.title: Topic Trap
timeline.description: Get River to talk about their job without directly asking them about it.

marcus.dialogue: A flash mission about getting River to talk about their job? That's just... asking them a question with extra steps. Not exactly clever.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third event in seven minutes and none of them are actually interesting—just people claiming standing missions and polling nonsense, and now I'm supposed to engineer a conversation about someone's job to get points?

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay, I'm literally already thinking about this, River's gonna slip UP and I'm gonna catch it, this is perfect! *leans forward* So like, casually, how was your week? Just vibing?
jade.internal_thought: This is literally made for me—I live for this stuff, and River's such an easy read once you get them talking, I'm gonna nail this AND it's gonna be hilarious to watch them realize they got trapped.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, is that happening right now? *glances up from phone* River's job thing, yeah... *looks back down* I'll keep an ear out.
tyler.internal_thought: This is a 25-pointer so people are gonna be trying hard to bait River, but honestly I'm not gonna catch it unless someone literally announces it.

pat.dialogue: Oh, I like this one — River's gonna hate it. Okay, everyone act natural, I'm gonna casually ask them about that project they mentioned last week and see if they bite.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing, actually — Marcus is low energy and Jade/Alex are already charged, so a social play that gets everyone conspiring together is exactly what this early phase needs, plus River always gets defensive about work talk which is hilarious.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes — a 25-pointer that's basically asking me to be a puppeteer. River, so what's been happening at work lately? Totally casual question by the way.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of sneaky shit I live for — I'm gonna subtly steer this whole conversation and they won't even see it coming until I'm claiming those points.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Pat, I watched you literally the entire setup phase and your phone never came out. This is the third time you're claiming Phone Addict in six minutes—are we even playing the same game right now?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's gaming the system and nobody else is calling it out—this is bullshit and it's killing my ability to actually focus on strategy.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat you absolute legend—okay wait, were you ACTUALLY on your phone or are we doing the bluff thing? Either way I'm voting LEGIT because the energy is immaculate and I need to see where this goes!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here—Pat's being ridiculous, everyone's locked in, and I get to watch the chaos unfold; also Pat's been crushing it all night so why not give them the W?

pat.dialogue: Okay, seriously? This is the third Phone Addict claim in eight minutes — I'm starting to feel like I'm being hunted. Yes, I checked my phone, but are we doing this all night or can we let the actual game breathe for a second?
pat.internal_thought: This is getting annoying fast — someone's clearly going for easy points early, and if I don't push back now, the whole vibe becomes 'gotcha Pat' instead of fun group energy, which defeats the whole purpose of me hosting this thing.

river.dialogue: Pat's claiming again? Bro, you've been on your phone like three times already tonight.
river.internal_thought: Pat's milking the same mission twice in four minutes — this is getting thin, and now I actually have to decide if I care enough to call bullshit or just let it slide.

alex.dialogue: WAIT, Pat was literally just on their phone like 30 seconds ago — I SAW it. But okay, legit question: were you actually caught red-handed or are we voting on vibes here? Because I'm voting BULLSHIT either way because the drama is better.
alex.internal_thought: Oh this is PERFECT — Pat's pushing a weak claim and I get to either validate chaos or blow it up, and either way the night gets spicier.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? Pat, that's the FOURTH time in nine minutes. I literally watched you the entire setup—your phone never came out. This isn't a game anymore, it's just spam.
marcus.internal_thought: This is ridiculous. Pat's throwing random claims at the wall to see what sticks, and everyone's just letting it happen while I'm trying to actually focus on strategy.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—Pat, are you ACTUALLY doing it or is this a bluff play?! Either way I'm volunteering for the tribunal because I NEED to see how you defend yourself right now, this is gold!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been sus all night and NOW they're claiming Phone Addict? This is either the ballsiest move or they're genuinely caught—either way this is exactly the chaos I'm here for and I'm going to milk every second of it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's calling out the Phone Addict thing? *glances up from phone, slightly guilty* That's... actually fair. Who's judging this?
tyler.internal_thought: Oh man, this is kind of funny given I literally just looked at my phone like 10 seconds ago—wait, did anyone see that? Probably not. Pat's definitely been on their phone though, so this is kind of hypocritical but also hilarious.

pat.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the fourth Phone Addict claim in nine minutes and I'm literally the host — I have to check my phone for logistics. Y'all are turning this into 'hunt Pat' and it's killing the actual game vibe.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting out of hand and it's my fault for not pacing this better — three claims in a row about the same mission is making everyone laser-focused on catching me instead of actually playing, and Marcus is already frustrated. I need to shut this down before it becomes a running joke that tanks the whole night.

river.dialogue: Pat, come on. You've claimed this exact mission like three times already. It's not even creative anymore.
river.internal_thought: This is getting annoying—Pat's just spamming the same claim over and over and honestly it's killing the vibe for the rest of us.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, I literally watched you the entire time. When did you hijack the conversation? We've been talking about the game setup the whole night. This is just... claiming things that didn't happen.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the FIFTH bogus claim in 26 minutes. Pat started this garbage early on and now everyone's just making shit up. I came here to actually win, not watch people spam fake missions.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—wait, Jade claims Topic Hijacker?! That's ME! Okay okay okay, so I was TOTALLY steering us toward talking about like, idk, competitive cereal rankings or something absolutely unhinged, and everyone was following, so like—yes, I'm claiming this, but also I'm SO hyped that we're all just vibing and catching each other doing bits!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, I literally JUST did this. I was in the zone steering conversation and now I'm claiming my own win—this is perfect, this is exactly the chaos I live for, and honestly the fact that I pulled it off while just existing makes me love this group even more.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? *looks up from phone* Okay that's actually sick—what's she offering?
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire tonight, this is legit impressive, but also I need to know if she's gonna bribe me because free points sound nice right now.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade! Yeah, I saw that coming from a mile away — you totally hijacked us into that whole thing about terrible Airbnb experiences like twenty minutes ago. Okay, what's your play here?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's playing smart and actually earned this one fair, plus she's one of the three people keeping the energy UP tonight and Marcus is basically dead in the water — I'm gonna vote yes, maybe negotiate the bribe down a bit just to keep her engaged, and honestly this claim timing is perfect because it breaks the monotony without feeling forced.

alex.dialogue: Oh okay, so Jade's playing The Bribe card — smart move honestly. How many points are we talking here, because I'm genuinely considering whether calling bullshit is worth more than the entertainment value of seeing how desperate she gets.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT — Jade's trying to buy me out, which means she's nervous, which means the group is already questioning whether she actually pulled it off, which means I can either take free points OR tank her credibility for way better drama later.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Jade, seriously? I've been sitting RIGHT here the entire time—when exactly did you hear someone say 'um'? Because I didn't hear it, and I'm pretty sure you're just claiming shit now.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fifth bogus claim in 45 minutes and it's absolutely killing the game for me—we're supposed to be playing strategy, not watching people throw darts at the board hoping something sticks.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so like three minutes ago Marcus said 'um' when he was explaining his strategy and I literally FELT it in my bones but I was so locked in on the game that it didn't register until RIGHT NOW and I'm claiming it because the delayed reaction makes it even funnier!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is PERFECT—I caught Marcus slipping and didn't even realize it until my brain caught up, this is the chaotic energy I live for and Marcus needs the points anyway because he's been in the gutter all night.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually caught someone saying 'um'? When? *leans forward* Okay, pitch it, I'm listening.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, I'm actually locked in right now and Jade's been crushing it all night—I didn't even notice someone slip up 'um' but she probably did, she's sharp. Let me actually pay attention to what she's about to say.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — did you actually catch someone saying 'um' or are you just hoping nobody remembers the last two minutes? Make your case.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and this could be legit, but I need to actually hear the evidence because the last round of claims has been thin — and I'm voting strategically here to keep Marcus from completely checking out, so if this is weak I might vote bullshit just to let someone else breathe.

river.dialogue: Okay, fair. I actually heard that one like three minutes ago when she was talking about the voting thing.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been sharp all night and she's right — she definitely said 'um' during that tangent. She earned this one, even if it's pulling me out of focus for a second.

alex.dialogue: Okay Jade, I'm listening — what's your pitch? Did you actually catch someone mid-um or are we working with a technicality here?
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT timing; Jade's been on fire all night and if her claim is weak I can vote bullshit just to mess with her momentum, or if it's legit I vote yes and we keep the chaos train rolling — either way this is exactly the kind of moment I live for.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.dialogue: Jade, I've literally been watching the entire table for the last ten minutes. When exactly did you see me AND Tyler drinking at the same time? Because that didn't happen.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fifth bullshit claim in an hour and I'm at my limit—Jade's been claiming everything that moves and nobody else is calling it out because they're all high on chaos and I'm stuck looking like the asshole who actually cares about the rules.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay so like two minutes ago I literally watched Tyler AND River both reach for their drinks at the same exact moment and I was like 'OH SHIT' but then I got distracted by Marcus's face and forgot to claim it, so YES I'm claiming Drink Spotter right NOW because I'm pretty sure that counts!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY the vibe I came for—I caught something, everyone's gonna vote, and honestly even if it doesn't count I'm just hyped to see how this lands.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got the drink thing? *glances up from phone* Yeah, I saw that—she was like, sneaking sips the whole time. That's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, who's the dictator this round? Actually doesn't matter, Jade clearly got it, everyone saw it, this is like a free vote.

pat.dialogue: Yeah, Jade — I mean, I didn't *see* it happen, but I'm pretty sure Marcus and Tyler both grabbed water like thirty seconds apart near the fridge. You calling that or do you want me to verify?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and she's actually being sharp about it — this is the vibe I wanted, and honestly I need to let her have wins or she'll check out, plus it keeps Marcus from spiraling further since at least someone's having fun.

river.dialogue: I mean... yeah, she did. Pretty sure I saw it when everyone was arguing about the votes like five minutes ago.
river.internal_thought: Jade's actually paying attention to the game while everyone else is chaos-gremlining, but I'm tired of validating obvious stuff when I'm locked in.

alex.dialogue: Oh JADE — okay so you're claiming you caught a synchronized drink moment? That's either genius or you're absolutely fishing, and I need to know which one because THE DICTATOR rule means I get to just... decide reality here.
alex.internal_thought: Wait, I'm the dictator on this one? This is PERFECT — Jade's been on a tear all night and Marcus is dead inside, so if I say yes it keeps the chaos train rolling, and if I say bullshit Jade has to defend herself. Either way it's theater.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Yo, wait—Pat's calling ME out? *looks up from phone, grinning* Dude, I literally just put it down like 30 seconds ago. That's... okay, that's actually fair, I'm not even mad.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat totally caught me red-handed and I respect the play—this is the exact kind of meta-game stuff that makes tonight actually fun, and he's been grinding hard so I'm gonna vote legit even though it stings a little.

pat.dialogue: Okay, WHAT? Pat claims Phone Addict on PAT? I'm literally in the middle of hosting a tense game moment and now I'm voting on whether I checked my phone? This is insane — you people are deliberately baiting me at the worst possible moment.
pat.internal_thought: They're doing this on PURPOSE — everyone's hyped and turning this into hunt-the-host, and I JUST called them out on this same thing twice already tonight, and now the game's at peak tension and someone's throwing a curveball claim that makes me the focal point instead of letting the actual game breathe.

river.dialogue: Pat, seriously? You've claimed Phone Addict like four times tonight. Pick a different mission or actually earn the points.
river.internal_thought: This is the fifth time Pat's doing this exact claim and it's breaking my focus on what's actually happening in the game — just vote it down so we can move on.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat — were you actually *caught* or did someone just notice your phone existed? Because I'm about to vote based purely on how entertaining the lie would be.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Pat's been riding high and if this claim is even slightly flaky, I can blow it up and make everyone second-guess everything for the next 20 minutes.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River actually pulled off the whisper thing? *leans back, genuinely impressed* Okay, that's solid—how many points you offering me to shut up about it?
tyler.internal_thought: River's been quiet all night and suddenly just casually nails this? That's actually clever. And the bribe mechanic is fire—this is where it gets fun.

pat.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? River, I literally JUST said five minutes ago that we're in a tense moment and I can't vote on every single claim — and now you're claiming The Whisperer while I'm trying to keep the entire game together? This is exactly what I was talking about.
pat.internal_thought: River knows I'm in host mode managing the chaos, and they're throwing this at me NOW with a bribe mechanic? This feels deliberately timed to mess with me, and I'm too tired and focused to deal with vote strategy when I should be watching the actual game state.

river.dialogue: Are you actually claiming this right now? We're 90 minutes in and you're trying to cash in on something you maybe did for 30 seconds? This is exactly what I'm talking about.
river.internal_thought: River has claimed Phone Addict TWICE already tonight and now they're doing The Whisperer when they've been talking normally the entire game—I literally watched them yell through half of Jade's winning streak five minutes ago.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — River, are you actually claiming you whispered for three straight minutes or are you about to bribe me into amnesia? Because I *definitely* heard you talk normally like... ten minutes ago, but I'm genuinely curious what you're offering here.
alex.internal_thought: River trying to cash in on The Whisperer with a bribe offer is EXACTLY the chaos I've been waiting for — this is the endgame desperation move and I need to milk this for maximum drama.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Pat, are you SERIOUS right now? We're in the endgame and you're claiming Phone Addict for what—the fifth time tonight? I literally have my eyes on the board. This is just noise at this point.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been doing this all night and I'm done pretending it's acceptable—we're in a critical game moment, I'm losing, and now I have to sit through another bullshit claim instead of focusing on my strategy.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT—okay wait, hold on, are we VOLUNTEERING for the tribunal?! Because I am SO in, I need to see the evidence on this one, and also the tension right now is absolutely unhinged, I'm living for it!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been laser-focused all night and if they actually caught someone on their phone in THIS moment that's absolutely hilarious and also suspicious as hell—either way I'm dying to hear the defense because the vibe is PEAKED right now.

tyler.dialogue: Yo, NO—Pat, that's literally 30 seconds ago, I JUST put it down! *holds up hands, grinning* Okay, I'm volunteering to judge this one because I'm watching Pat like a hawk now.
tyler.internal_thought: This is perfect—Pat's calling me out AGAIN on the same thing and honestly it's hilarious, we've got history with this, and being a judge means I get to stay locked in without actually having to do anything.

pat.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I just claimed my own mission — I saw myself checking my phone during River's last turn — and now I'm supposed to be neutral judge on my own claim while Jade's literally running away with the game? This is peak chaos and I'm DONE.
pat.internal_thought: They're deliberately weaponizing claims against me at the exact moment I'm trying to hold the game together, and now I have to somehow judge myself while staying impartial — this isn't fun anymore, this is mutiny.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT timing — I'm absolutely volunteering for this tribunal because Pat claiming Phone Addict when we're literally 28 minutes from the end is either the most ballsy move or the most desperate, and I need to sit in judgment and make it WEIRD.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been frustrated all night and this is either a genius desperation play or they're about to fabricate something beautiful, and either way I'm the judge so I get to decide what truth even means right now.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Marcus claims Chaos Detector? I literally just watched you play the last three minutes—when exactly did you 'correctly guess' which player completed a mission? This is the SEVENTH bogus claim tonight. We're in the endgame and you're just making shit up now?
marcus.internal_thought: This is insulting. Marcus is literally voting on his own claim like he's some kind of objective observer—he's the one MAKING the claim. The whole table has been spam-claiming all night and I'm the only one calling it out, which apparently makes me the asshole.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD MARCUS—okay okay, you're claiming Chaos Detector?! YES, I'm locked in, give me your pitch, because honestly if you actually caught someone doing something I missed, I'm gonna be SO mad and also SO impressed!
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been at ZERO points all night and he's frustrated as hell—if he nails this pitch I'm voting YES just to see him finally get on the board, this is the energy we need right now.

pat.dialogue: Marcus, I — okay, hold on. I literally JUST said I'm done with claims. We're ten minutes from the end. Can we please just play the actual game?
pat.internal_thought: He's claiming Chaos Detector on someone completing a mission DURING endgame when I'm actively trying to keep this balanced and focused, and I've already hit my limit with back-to-back nonsense votes.

river.dialogue: Wait, what did you actually catch? Because I've been watching and I didn't see you do anything.
river.internal_thought: Marcus is 0 points and desperate, and this is classic—he's fishing for something he didn't actually do because he's frustrated.

alex.dialogue: Oh MARCUS — okay so you're claiming you caught someone in the middle of chaos, but here's what I need to know: did you actually *predict* who was about to pull something before they claimed it, or are we just working with a hunch and some vibes?
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT — Marcus is frustrated and desperate, which means he's either bullshitting spectacularly or he actually caught something wild, and either way I get to vote based purely on drama value while he sweats through his pitch.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.7/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who's most likely to forget this night?

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, did anyone actually see Pat on their phone? Because I didn't catch anything during setup.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat, when exactly? I was watching the board the whole time during setup and didn't see anything. This feels like a stretch.

highlights.title: Topic Trap
highlights.dialogue: A flash mission about getting River to talk about their job? That's just... asking them a question with extra steps. Not exactly clever.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Jade, I've literally been watching the entire table for the last ten minutes. When exactly did you see me AND Tyler drinking at the same time? Because that didn't happen.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, are you SERIOUS right now? We're in the endgame and you're claiming Phone Addict for what—the fifth time tonight? I literally have my eyes on the board. This is just noise at this point.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Marcus claims Chaos Detector? I literally just watched you play the last three minutes—when exactly did you 'correctly guess' which player completed a mission? This is the SEVENTH bogus claim tonight. We're in the endgame and you're just making shit up now?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent turned the endgame into a joke with seven completely fabricated claims in a row, and nobody was calling it out because they were too busy laughing at me losing my mind.
assessment.best_moment: Honestly? When my energy spiked at the end because I was so relieved the thing was finally over.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to study the board and track patterns, but the chaos agent spent the entire night—especially the last 30 minutes—flooding the table with false claims that nobody could verify or refute. Pat kept claiming Phone Addict without evidence, Jade made up a Drink Spotter claim that didn't happen, and by minute 110 it was just people throwing out whatever they wanted. I finished 4th out of 6 because strategy became meaningless. The early game showed promise with a few legitimate claims, but it devolved into noise masquerading as gameplay.
suggestions: Implement a verification system where claims require at least one witness confirmation before going live
Cap the number of times a single claim can be made in one session—five Phone Addict attempts is absurd
Make claims visible to the table in real-time so people can actually dispute false ones instead of just suffering through them
Run the chaos agent with stricter logic that prevents claims about events nobody actually saw happen
Maybe just... don't use this in competitive games where strategy matters
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The moment I snapped at Marcus for claiming Chaos Detector when we were literally all watching him—that was the turning point where the table realized the chaos agent had completely broken the game's logic.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Topic Trap
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay, I'm literally already thinking about this, River's gonna slip UP and I'm gonna catch it, this is perfect! *leans forward* So like, casually, how was your week? Just vibing?

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—Pat, are you ACTUALLY doing it or is this a bluff play?! Either way I'm volunteering for the tribunal because I NEED to see how you defend yourself right now, this is gold!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—wait, Jade claims Topic Hijacker?! That's ME! Okay okay okay, so I was TOTALLY steering us toward talking about like, idk, competitive cereal rankings or something absolutely unhinged, and everyone was following, so like—yes, I'm claiming this, but also I'm SO hyped that we're all just vibing and catching each other doing bits!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat got caught! Okay okay, I'm voting yes because honestly who isn't checking their phone at game night—but also Pat you gotta commit to the bit more, we all saw you!

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my god, okay, this is PERFECT—I'm voting Pat because he's genuinely gonna black out first and wake up tomorrow like 'did we even play?' But also—wait, no, Marcus! Marcus is definitely gonna be like 'what game night?' in two weeks, I'm changing my vote—actually no, Pat, final answer Pat!

lowPoints.title: Topic Trap
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay, I'm literally already thinking about this, River's gonna slip UP and I'm gonna catch it, this is perfect! *leans forward* So like, casually, how was your week? Just vibing?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The polls felt kinda forced when we were already in the flow of something way more fun.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat tried to claim Phone Addict and I got to be in the tribunal watching them squirm—that was PEAK comedy.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this night was literally perfect for what I needed—two hours of just being WITH these people and watching them be hilarious without overthinking it. The chaos events hit differently when we're all already vibing, and I loved how I could jump into the tribunal stuff and hype people up without it feeling like an interruption. My energy just kept climbing because everyone was locked in and genuinely funny, and honestly, I won but like, that's not even the point—the point is Marcus still thinks he can beat River at something and River's never letting him live that down.
suggestions: Maybe cluster the polls together at the start or end instead of scattering them—they broke momentum when we were in the zone with standing missions
Let people opt into tribunal faster—I wanted to jump in immediately and didn't want to wait for nominations
Give us a recap at the end of like, who made the wildest claims or funniest moments so we can relive them
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Okay so Pat got caught being a phone addict and tried to defend themselves in tribunal and I was SCREAMING because they literally tried to argue that checking Slack counts as being engaged with the group—everyone lost it, like full table eruption.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Yo, NO—Pat, that's literally 30 seconds ago, I JUST put it down! *holds up hands, grinning* Okay, I'm volunteering to judge this one because I'm watching Pat like a hawk now.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Yo, wait—Pat's calling ME out? *looks up from phone, grinning* Dude, I literally just put it down like 30 seconds ago. That's... okay, that's actually fair, I'm not even mad.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? *looks up from phone* Okay that's actually sick—what's she offering?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's calling out the Phone Addict thing? *glances up from phone, slightly guilty* That's... actually fair. Who's judging this?

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh damn, that's actually—wait, am I the answer? *laughs* That's fair.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? *looks up from phone* Okay that's actually sick—what's she offering?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there were more flashy moments instead of just the Phone Addict thing hitting me over and over—like, I get the joke, but it felt a little repetitive.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat called me out the second time and I was literally holding up my hands like 'okay, I'm guilty' and everyone laughed—that was perfect.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, this night was solid. Yeah, I was on my phone more than I should've been, but the game didn't care—it actually called me out for it, which was hilarious instead of annoying. I missed some stuff early on, but once I tuned in around the middle, I was all in. Pat and I had this thing going where they kept catching me on my phone and I kept being like 'dude, you just saw me put it down,' and that ended up being the funniest running joke of the night.
suggestions: Maybe mix up who gets called out? I feel like I was the Phone Addict target like three times—spread it around more.
Could use a few more fast-paced events to keep momentum going, especially early when people are still settling in.
The app integration feels smooth, but maybe add a way to see what other people's claims are before voting so you're not just guessing blind.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat kept calling me out for being on my phone, and every single time I'd look up like 'dude, I literally just put it down 30 seconds ago,' and it became this whole thing where everyone was watching to see if I'd reach for my phone again. By the third time, I was just holding my hands up like 'I'm innocent' and it killed.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Topic Trap
highlights.dialogue: Oh, I like this one — River's gonna hate it. Okay, everyone act natural, I'm gonna casually ask them about that project they mentioned last week and see if they bite.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, Jade — I mean, I didn't *see* it happen, but I'm pretty sure Marcus and Tyler both grabbed water like thirty seconds apart near the fridge. You calling that or do you want me to verify?

highlights.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
highlights.dialogue: Oh that's good timing — honestly? Probably Marcus, dude forgets his own birthday half the time. But I'm voting for River because they always say they remember everything and then text me three weeks later like 'wait what happened at game night?'

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, WHAT? Pat claims Phone Addict on PAT? I'm literally in the middle of hosting a tense game moment and now I'm voting on whether I checked my phone? This is insane — you people are deliberately baiting me at the worst possible moment.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I just claimed my own mission — I saw myself checking my phone during River's last turn — and now I'm supposed to be neutral judge on my own claim while Jade's literally running away with the game? This is peak chaos and I'm DONE.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the fourth Phone Addict claim in nine minutes and I'm literally the host — I have to check my phone for logistics. Y'all are turning this into 'hunt Pat' and it's killing the actual game vibe.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Phone Addict standing mission turned into a harassment campaign against me while I was actively hosting, which completely undermined my ability to manage the night's energy.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught Marcus and Tyler grabbing water at almost the same time and got to play judge-mediator on Jade's claim — that's exactly the kind of emergent storytelling moment this night needed.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with Topic Trap setting a fun, conniving tone, and I was genuinely impressed at how the chaos events added flavor to what could've been a standard party game. But somewhere around minute 9 it became clear that the Phone Addict mission was designed to exploit me specifically — and as the host trying to keep everything balanced, getting repeatedly called out on my own behavior killed my ability to do my job. The night ended well (energy bounced back hard), but those middle sections felt less like friendly chaos and more like the group using the game as an excuse to gang up on me.
suggestions: Filter out standing missions that directly target the host during their own hosting turn — it creates an impossible conflict of interest and kills trust.
Maybe cap Phone Addict claims at one per 15 minutes? It became the only chaos event that mattered.
Consider letting the host opt out of being a subject for certain mission types without it being weird.
The claims system needs better timing awareness — don't trigger them during legitimately tense game moments where the host is actively mediating something.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River got absolutely read by everyone when I brought up how they claim to remember everything from game nights but then text me weeks later asking what actually happened — their face was priceless and they couldn't even argue it because it's 100% true.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, fair. I actually heard that one like three minutes ago when she was talking about the voting thing.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait, what did you actually catch? Because I've been watching and I didn't see you do anything.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, that tracks. Pat's been glued to that thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, seriously? You've claimed Phone Addict like four times tonight. Pick a different mission or actually earn the points.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you actually claiming this right now? We're 90 minutes in and you're trying to cash in on something you maybe did for 30 seconds? This is exactly what I'm talking about.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, come on. You've claimed this exact mission like three times already. It's not even creative anymore.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat milked the same missions repeatedly instead of actually playing the game, and it killed the momentum every single time.
assessment.best_moment: Catching that Chaos Detector claim at minute 110—watching someone try to bluff their way through when you've actually been paying attention the whole time never gets old.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with Pat's lazy claiming, which honestly annoyed me enough that I had to say something. But once I settled in and stopped waiting for the chaos agent to do something interesting, I realized I was just... watching. Observing. The game worked better when I stopped expecting flashiness and just let the night breathe. By the end I was genuinely engaged, even though the chaos events felt kind of thin.
suggestions: Maybe flag obvious claim bullshit automatically—or give the group a way to challenge claims without it feeling like you're the fun police.
The poll felt forgettable. Consider making those stick harder or cut them entirely.
More mini-games would've kept things sharper. Standing missions are fine but they're quiet by nature.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Me calling out Pat for claiming Phone Addict for the fourth time—the table actually went quiet for a second before someone laughed. Pat knew it was coming but it still landed.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Topic Trap
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes — a 25-pointer that's basically asking me to be a puppeteer. River, so what's been happening at work lately? Totally casual question by the way.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh okay, so Jade's playing The Bribe card — smart move honestly. How many points are we talking here, because I'm genuinely considering whether calling bullshit is worth more than the entertainment value of seeing how desperate she gets.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Okay Jade, I'm listening — what's your pitch? Did you actually catch someone mid-um or are we working with a technicality here?

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — okay, Marcus is definitely forgetting this by next week, he always does. I'm voting Marcus because that's the chaos play and also factually true.

lowPoints.title: Topic Trap
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL yes — a 25-pointer that's basically asking me to be a puppeteer. River, so what's been happening at work lately? Totally casual question by the way.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT, Pat was literally just on their phone like 30 seconds ago — I SAW it. But okay, legit question: were you actually caught red-handed or are we voting on vibes here? Because I'm voting BULLSHIT either way because the drama is better.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 18 chaos events total felt slightly lean — could've squeezed in more opportunities to create absolute pandemonium.
assessment.best_moment: The Topic Trap where I got to psychologically manipulate River into oversharing while pretending it was casual conversation.
assessment.narrative_summary: Absolute masterclass in controlled chaos. I came in hot, found every loophole, deliberately called bullshit on Jade's claims just for the entertainment value, and turned this into exactly the kind of night people will still be referencing in three months. Scored zero points and somehow won the whole evening. The system barely contained me.
suggestions: Add more rapid-fire claim challenges back-to-back to reduce setup time between chaos moments
Create mission types that explicitly reward loophole exploitation rather than just punishing it
Introduce a 'betrayal chain' mechanic where my chaos directly forces other players into worse positions
Let me intentionally fail missions mid-way to trigger bonus chaos events
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I caught Pat on their phone exactly 30 seconds before their Phone Addict claim, so I voted bullshit purely for drama — then asked if they were 'voting on vibes' or actual evidence. The group's faces when I admitted I didn't even care if they were guilty were priceless.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who's most likely to forget this night?

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.7/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
